codeReview.summary: The feature removes the old Gist-ID UI and switches the frontend plumbing to pairing codes, but it still misses a required part of the first-enable UX: the settings panel does not show the newly generated code until the user leaves and reopens settings or the config refreshes elsewhere.

issues.description: The pairing-code display renders only when `config.syncBeaconEnabled && hasPairingCode`, but `handleToggle()` only saves `{ syncBeaconEnabled: true }` and never updates local state with a generated code. The actual code is generated later inside `sync-beacon-context.tsx` during background `pushBeacon()` and persisted via `setConfig`, so immediately after first enable the settings panel still shows no code even though the expected behavior says first enable should auto-generate a pairing code and display it. This leaves the primary pairing UX incomplete on the first interaction.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill tells workers to do browser verification by starting the dev server, but it does not call out that many desktop-only flows (including Sync Beacon settings backed by Tauri commands/config updates) cannot be fully exercised in browser mode. This worker marked the procedure as fully followed after verifying only the figma-capture preview, which cannot validate the real first-enable pairing-code generation flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 6 in .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md requires browser verification on `http://localhost:4158`; .factory/library/environment.md notes browser mode is UI-only with no git ops; the transcript verified the figma-capture preview instead of the live Tauri-backed settings flow.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `c715a69`, and the relevant frontend files. The Gist-ID UI is removed and pairing-code wiring is mostly in place, but the feature still fails a core requirement: first enable does not immediately display the generated pairing code in settings, so the simplified pairing UX is incomplete. Report saved for scrutiny follow-up.
